name: gparted
title: gparted
summary: gparted - GNOME Partition Editor for manipulating disk partitions.

commands.name: gparted
commands.description: GNOME Partition Editor for manipulating disk partitions.
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# man gparted
 GPARTED(8)                       GParted Manual                      GPARTED(8)
 
 NAME
    gparted - GNOME Partition Editor for manipulating disk partitions.
 
 SYNOPSIS
    gparted [device]...
 
 DESCRIPTION
    The  gparted application is the GNOME partition editor for creating, reor-
    ganizing, and deleting disk partitions.
 
    A disk device can be subdivided into one or more partitions.  The  gparted
    application enables you to change the partition organization on a disk de-
    vice while preserving the contents of the partition.
 
    With gparted you can accomplish the following tasks:
    - Create a partition table on a disk device.
    - Enable and disable partition flags such as boot and hidden.
    -  Perform  actions  with partitions such as create, delete, resize, move,
    check, label, copy, and paste.
 
    More documentation can be found in the application help manual, and online
    at:
    https://gparted.org
 
 EXAMPLES
    You can run gparted from a command line and specify one or more  disk  de-
    vices.
 
    For  example,  to start gparted with the devices /dev/sda and /dev/sdc you
    would use the following command:
 
    gparted /dev/sda /dev/sdc
 
 NOTES
    Editing partitions has the potential to cause LOSS of DATA.
 
    The gparted application is designed to enable you to edit partitions while
    reducing the risk of data loss.  The application is carefully  tested  and
    is  used  by  the GParted project team.  However, loss of data might occur
    due to software bugs, hardware problems, or power failure.
 
    You can help to reduce the risk of data loss by not mounting or unmounting
    partitions outside of the gparted application while gparted is running.
 
    You are advised to BACKUP your DATA before using the gparted application.
 
 REPORTING BUGS
    Report bugs at:
    https://gparted.org/bugs.php
 
 AUTHOR
    Manual page written by Curtis Gedak <gedakc@users.sf.net>
 
 SEE ALSO
    parted(8), fdisk(8), mkfs(8), ntfsprogs(8)
 
 gparted                          Jan 16th, 2011                      GPARTED(8)

local_help.man:
GPARTED(8)                       GParted Manual                      GPARTED(8)

NAME
     gparted - GNOME Partition Editor for manipulating disk partitions.

SYNOPSIS
     gparted [device]...

DESCRIPTION
     The  gparted application is the GNOME partition editor for creating, reor-
     ganizing, and deleting disk partitions.

     A disk device can be subdivided into one or more partitions.  The  gparted
     application enables you to change the partition organization on a disk de-
     vice while preserving the contents of the partition.

     With gparted you can accomplish the following tasks:
     - Create a partition table on a disk device.
     - Enable and disable partition flags such as boot and hidden.
     -  Perform  actions  with partitions such as create, delete, resize, move,
     check, label, copy, and paste.

     More documentation can be found in the application help manual, and online
     at:
     https://gparted.org

EXAMPLES
     You can run gparted from a command line and specify one or more  disk  de-
     vices.

     For  example,  to start gparted with the devices /dev/sda and /dev/sdc you
     would use the following command:

     gparted /dev/sda /dev/sdc

NOTES
     Editing partitions has the potential to cause LOSS of DATA.

     The gparted application is designed to enable you to edit partitions while
     reducing the risk of data loss.  The application is carefully  tested  and
     is  used  by  the GParted project team.  However, loss of data might occur
     due to software bugs, hardware problems, or power failure.

     You can help to reduce the risk of data loss by not mounting or unmounting
     partitions outside of the gparted application while gparted is running.

     You are advised to BACKUP your DATA before using the gparted application.

REPORTING BUGS
     Report bugs at:
     https://gparted.org/bugs.php

AUTHOR
     Manual page written by Curtis Gedak <gedakc@users.sf.net>

SEE ALSO
     parted(8), fdisk(8), mkfs(8), ntfsprogs(8)

gparted                          Jan 16th, 2011                      GPARTED(8)
local_help.apropos: gparted (8)          - GNOME Partition Editor for manipulating disk partitions.

binary_metadata.file_type: POSIX shell script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.suggests: dmraid, dmsetup, dosfstools, e2fsprogs, gpart, jfsutils, kpartx, mtools, ntfs-3g, reiser4progs, reiserfsprogs, udftools, xfsprogs, exfatprogs, yelp
apt_metadata.description:
GNOME partition editor
GParted uses libparted to detect and manipulate devices and partition
tables while several (optional) filesystem tools provide support for
filesystems not included in libparted.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use gparted because gparted - GNOME Partition Editor for manipulating disk partitions.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `gparted [device]...` as documented upstream for gparted.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `gparted /dev/sda /dev/sdc` as documented upstream for gparted.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair gparted with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `gparted --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: gparted - GNOME Partition Editor for manipulating disk partitions.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install gparted. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `gparted [device]...`. Further invocations from upstream: `gparted /dev/sda /dev/sdc`. gparted - GNOME Partition Editor for manipulating disk partitions.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: gparted - GNOME Partition Editor for manipulating disk partitions.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: gparted - GNOME Partition Editor for manipulating disk partitions.
